stories.title: Gold: Near term capped, medium term constructive – OCBC
stories.summary: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong report that Gold has come under pressure as rising global yields, higher real rates and renewed inflation concerns reduce expectations for near-term Fed cuts. ETF outflows and stress-driven liquidation are adding to downside.

stories.title: USD/INR hits all-time highs as Middle East conflicts weakens Indian Rupee
stories.summary: The Indian Rupee (INR) extends its downfall against the US Dollar (USD) at the start of the holiday-shortened week. Indian markets will remain closed on Thursday due to Shri Ram Navami.

stories.title: EUR/USD: Downside risks after hawkish ECB repricing – ING
stories.summary: ING strategist Francesco Pesole highlights that aggressive repricing of European Central Bank tightening, with three hikes now fully priced, may have gone too far.

stories.title: ECB’s Kazimir: Every future meeting is open and live
stories.summary: European Central Bank (ECB) Governing Council member and Governor of the National Bank of Slovakia (NBS) Peter Kazimir delivers remarks on the monetary policy outlook during the European trading session on Monday.

stories.title: Silver price today: Silver falls, according to FXStreet data
stories.summary: Silver prices (XAG/USD) fell on Monday, according to FXStreet data. Silver trades at $64.58 per troy ounce, down 4.82% from the $67.85 it cost on Friday.

stories.title: AUD: RBA hikes and solid fundamentals support – HSBC
stories.summary: HSBC economists report that the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) raised its cash rate to 4.10% in March, marking a second consecutive hike driven mainly by domestic capacity constraints and inflation concerns. They still see additional tightening as likely, possibly in May.

stories.title: Oil: Higher prices with prolonged Gulf disruption – Rabobank
stories.summary: Rabobank strategists Michael Every, Florence Schmit and Joe DeLaura note that Brent crude has surged as Middle East conflict intensifies and the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed.

stories.title: Gold bounces off 200-day SMA, four-month low as bears turn cautious amid oversold conditions
stories.summary: Gold (XAU/USD) bounces off a technically significant 200-day Simple Moving Average (SMA) during the first half of the European session on Monday and trims a part of its heavy intraday losses to a four-month low.

stories.title: US: Energy insulation but higher inflation pressures – Deutsche Bank
stories.summary: Deutsche Bank economists argue that the US is relatively shielded on growth from the current energy shock due to its net energy exporter status, but still faces a notable inflation impulse.

stories.title: Gold: Technical support tested as liquidation continues – MUFG
stories.summary: MUFG’s Senior Currency Analyst Lee Hardman reports that Gold and other precious metals are under pressure despite heightened geopolitical risks.

stories.title: USD/KRW trades near 1,510 after pulling back from 17-year highs
stories.summary: USD/KRW trades around 1,510.00 during the European hours after retreating from a 17-year high of 1,516.76 reached earlier on Monday. Heightened risk aversion triggered foreign outflows of 1.8 trillion Won, putting downward pressure on the South Korean Won (KRW).

stories.title: Oil: Demand elasticity estimates point to limited relief – Societe Generale
stories.summary: Societe Generale economists argue that Oil demand remains structurally inelastic despite sharp price gains. They estimate short‑run crude demand elasticity at –0.024, implying around 1.2 mb/d of lost demand so far, and warn that a move toward $150/bbl could destroy up to 2.7 mb/d

stories.title: EUR/USD Price Forecast: 20-day EMA acts as key barrier, sees more downside below 1.1400
stories.summary: The EUR/USD pair weakens as the US Dollar (USD) trades higher due to escalating Middle East conflicts, trading 0.3% lower to near 1.1535 during the European trading session on Monday.

stories.title: GBP: Resilience fading as BoE repricing bites – Commerzbank
stories.summary: Commerzbank’s Michael Pfister highlights that the Pound has held up despite a weak UK real economy, persistent inflation and strained public finances, helped by reduced political risk and aggressive Bank of England (BoE) repricing from cuts to multiple hikes.

stories.title: Silver Price Forecast: XAG/USD trades near $64.50 after rebounding from 15-week lows
stories.summary: Silver price (XAG/USD) continues its losing streak for the fifth consecutive day, down by nearly 5%, and is trading around $64.60 per troy ounce during the European hours on Monday.

stories.title: USD/JPY: 160.0 level in focus as intervention risk shifts – ING
stories.summary: Francesco Pesole at ING expects Japan’s CPI to slow further due to subsidies, though core‑core inflation should stay above 2%, keeping the Bank of Japan (BoJ) cautious about ruling out further hikes.

stories.title: USD: Haven bid challenged by hawkish G10 wall – DBS
stories.summary: DBS Group Research economist Philip Weeargues that although risk aversion from the Iran conflict usually supports the Dollar, this time the USD’s haven appeal may prove short-lived. He points to a Fed pause versus G10 rate hikes and pressure on US Treasuries from rising fiscal co

stories.title: AUD: RBA hike backs bullish outlook – BNY
stories.summary: BNY’s Head of Markets Macro Strategy Bob Savage notes that Australian data and policy are central for the Australian Dollar (AUD), with February CPI seen as critical to validate current rate-hike pricing and a constructive AUD view.

stories.title: Canadian Dollar outperforms major peers amid surging oil prices
stories.summary: The Canadian Dollar (CAD) trades higher against its major currency peers, but is marginally down to near 1.3735 against the US Dollar (USD) during the European trading session on Monday.

stories.title: US Dollar Index advances to near 100.00 due to strengthening Fed hawkish stance
stories.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which measures the value of the US Dollar (USD) against six major currencies, extends its gains for the second successive session and is trading around 99.80 during the early European hours on Monday.

stories.title: Gold bear market and sub-$50K BTC: Five things to know in Bitcoin this week
stories.summary: Bitcoin traders kept sub-$50,000 BTC price targets in play as gold entered a bear market over Iran and oil-supply instability.

stories.title: ZachXBT says fake X accounts used viral war content to drive crypto scams
stories.summary: ZachXBT said the network of fake X accounts used AI to impersonate influencers and post sensational content, generating millions of views and six-figure profits from crypto scams.

stories.title: Sweden’s H100 targets Norwegian firms in all-stock Bitcoin deal
stories.summary: H100 signed a letter of intent to acquire two Bitcoin treasury companies and their BTC holdings, which could make it the second-largest Bitcoin treasury company in Europe.

stories.title: Bithumb seeks to reappoint CEO despite recent controversies: Report
stories.summary: Bithumb was reportedly given a six-month partial suspension by South Korea’s Financial Intelligence Unit for alleged anti-money laundering failures.

stories.title: Global turmoil pushes uptake of decentralized messengers, social media
stories.summary: Bitchat saw a spike in downloads during protests in Madagascar, Nepal, Indonesia and Iran over the last year, and global unrest could see more cases like it.

stories.title: Hong Kong’s Boyaa Interactive eyes $70M crypto treasury expansion
stories.summary: Boyaa Interactive International is the 23rd-largest Bitcoin treasury and the third-largest in Asia, behind Japan’s Metaplanet and China’s Next Technology Holding.

stories.title: Mark Zuckerberg is building an AI agent to help run Meta
stories.summary: The Meta co-founder is reportedly working on a personal AI agent to bypass management layers as Meta pushes employees to adopt agentic tools.

stories.title: Crypto, stocks fall as oil whipsaws after Iran vows response to Trump threat
stories.summary: Bitcoin and Asian markets dropped on Monday as Iran escalated threats to strike critical infrastructure in the Gulf.

stories.title: Agentic AI commerce may spell the end of internet ads: a16z Crypto
stories.summary: “The internet is civilization’s town square, and the economic contract is now obsolete,” wrote Sam Ragsdale on an a16z Crypto blog on Sunday.

stories.title: Michael Saylor hints at Bitcoin buy as Strategy’s stack slips 10% into the red
stories.summary: Michael Saylor’s Bitcoin-holding company Strategy has already purchased more than $2.9 billion worth of the cryptocurrency this month.
